justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with explicit math showing 200+150+800=1150ms exceeds 500ms inbound timeout, (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence described — service continues processing after upstream disconnects, wasting compute, (3) Outbound timeout > inbound timeout relationship explicitly called out ('outbound timeouts are effectively decorative', 1000ms×3 vs 500ms), (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, increasing upstream timeout, partial enrichment with async backfill, circuit breakers, context cancellation propagation, (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional math breakdown showing sequential p99 sum (1150ms) vs inbound timeout (500ms), including p50 estimation showing even median is dangerously close
Correctly identified that InventoryService p99 alone (800ms) exceeds the inbound timeout — even parallelization doesn't fully solve it
Thorough treatment of orphaned requests: identified context cancellation propagation as a must-have, noted wasted compute on responses nobody will read
Smart observation that lack of retries is actually correct given the timeout budget — adding retries would make things worse
Excellent escalation routing: architecture decision to MJ, domain question about partial enrichment to Bird
Health check analysis identifying liveness vs readiness distinction is a valuable operational insight beyond the rubric requirements
Prioritized recommendations clearly separated into blocking must-haves and next-iteration should-haves
